channel.title: Deep-Rl on Research Portfolio
channel.description: Recent content in Deep-Rl on Research Portfolio

item.title: Reconstructing Modern Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Reinforcement learning papers often present a misleading picture of the field. Algorithms appear as independent inventions — DQN, PPO, SAC, HER — each with its own notation, diagrams, and benchmarks. From a distance this looks like steady progress. From close up it feels like memorizing a zoo.

This series takes a different position:

Modern reinforcement learning is not a sequence of new ideas. It is a sequence of repairs.

Every major algorithm exists because a simpler learning rule failed under a specific violated assumption. Once you identify the assumption that broke, the algorithm becomes obvious. Once you don’t, the field looks mysterious.
